reviews.review_text:
I'm really glad I finally had the chance to play the game I had heard so much about ever since it came out. I completely understand now why so many people love Ellie and Joel. As a father who has lost a daughter, I felt every painful moment Joel experienced, and I fully embraced his absolute need to protect Ellie at all costs.

This isn't a new feeling for me in gaming either. I felt it with Elizabeth in BioShock Infinite, I felt it with the Little Sisters in BioShock and BioShock 2, and I'm sure I'll feel it again with Diana in Pragmata. Games like this always pull me in, and this one absolutely did.

The story itself is not overly complex, but it hits all the right emotional beats. The world is incredibly rich with detail. Every building, bedroom, and house feels lived in, like someone actually worked there or called it home before everything fell apart. The attention to detail throughout the game is honestly astounding.

On top of that, the voice acting is top notch. Ashley Johnson and Troy Baker are phenomenal as Ellie and Joel. The story, world, and acting are all amazing.

The gameplay did take some getting used to, though. I really struggled with aiming accurately using a controller and often ended up using my mouse to aim and shoot while keeping my left hand on the controller for movement. Obviously that is far from ideal, but it's the exact same thing I had to do when I played Uncharted. I don't think that's necessarily the game's fault as much as my own inability to shoot well with a controller. That said, I never had those issues playing Horizon Forbidden West, so maybe it is the game and not me. :)

The last thing I'll say is that I don't recommend playing the Left Behind DLC immediately after finishing the main game. I really wish I had let the ending sit with me for a while before jumping into it. Honestly, parts of it feel like they should have just been included in the base game anyway. The sections involving recovering medical supplies felt important enough to belong in the main story, and the stuff with Riley was already hinted at enough that it didn't really need further explanation. Also, the Halloween store section was just kind of weird to play through.

Overall, I completely understand why this game has the reputation it does. Even with some gameplay frustrations here and there, the emotional connection I had with Joel and Ellie carried the entire experience for me, and it's one of those stories that is probably going to stick with me for a very long time.

The Last of Us Part I.

I've heard all about how great this game was since I was in Uni, back in 2013 or so. Of course, having no PlayStation 3, I could never play it until it released on PC. I got the game as soon as it released on Steam, but my PC at the time couldn't play it very well, so I put it on hold until I got a better PC, and finally, a year after getting a new PC, I gave this game a proper go.

Boy, those people saying this game was great were right. Of course, what I played is a remastered version with enhanced graphics (and also, several performance patches later on PC), but even from a storytelling perspective, which hasn't changed since its original release, it's a fantastic experience. Now I know some people might complain about the "cinematic" quality of PlayStation games, but personally, I love it, and it's not like this game lacks in gameplay.

So, the first thing I'd like to talk about is the one I enjoyed the most, which is the story. By this point everyone who's slightly familiar with the franchise knows the basic premise: a Cordyceps fungus has made the jump to infect humans, turning them into zombie-like creatures. After the initial outbreak, and the tragedy that befalls to our protagonist Joel, we skip 20 years into the future, where he's kind of a bitter man and is tasked to escort Ellie across the country. What follows is a story in which both characters get to know and trust each other, and honestly an interesting exploration of human nature. Sure, the infected are a threat, but so are some of the humans fighting for survival. Now, again, I know most people familiar with it will probably know the plot already, but I still think the later details are best left for one to experience on their own. Just trust me in that this is an excellent story.

Gameplay wise, I'll admit there were points when the game frustrated me. While a good chunk of the time will be spent exploring all these post-apocalyptic towns and cities, there's obviously combat encounters in almost every chapter. Most of them can be completed using a stealth approach, although some require you to fight head-on. What I found frustrating is the way weapons handle: there's a sway to them that might make it hard to hit enemies, and even human enemies might take several shots to go down. Now, I'm not saying I think the combat system is bad; on the contrary, it's brutal, and it provides a rewarding feeling whenever you manage to complete an encounter. Besides, while I mentioned the weapon sway, there's several different upgrades that can help you in combat, both for your weapons, and your character as well.

When it comes to graphics, it looks spectacular. Lots of details in the environment, and there's a certain beauty to the world, even with its post-apocalyptic setting. Going through ruined cities and areas reclaimed by nature left me wanting to pull out a camera and just take pictures of the whole thing. Even the creature design for the infected is superb. Terrifyingly beautiful, I'd say. Sound design doesn't fall behind either, whether it's the dialogue (the chemistry between Joel and Ellie is top-notch), or audio in general. And the soundtrack, oh, it's so good; Gustavo Santaolalla was a fantastic choice for it.

I gotta say, I was pleasantly surprised with this game, even knowing that I was going into what's already considered a great experience by many. And even if people might say it's too cinematic at times, I'd contrast that by saying it's also one of the "gamey"-est games I've played in recent years. I say that because, once you beat it, you can unlock so much stuff, from concept art and character skins, to actual gameplay modifiers like unlimited ammo, and even silly stuff like 8-bit audio. For a serious game, it also knows how to provide fun for repeated playthroughs.

Overall, I had a great time with it, and so I can easily recommend it. Now I just have to make the time to play Part II.

Finally played The Last of Us Part I and I completely understand why people love it so much. Amazing story, incredible atmosphere, and characters that actually feel human. I enjoyed every minute of it.

Do not fall for all the troll-bait and negative review-bombing! You will get lost in the game's atmosphere, I was completly invested in the story, spent hours without even realising. Absolutely Recommended.
its very good game the story is so good i wish i could re experience the game

Oh man...Since I am a PC gamer I haven't played The Last of us when it came out, but now with the remaster I'm doing both parts.

So...to be short...It's a heavily story driven game, do not buy it if you except brainless action all the time. That said, the game is best played stealthy but of course you can go guns blazing and it feels good. With the updated graphics it just looks awesome even now in 2026...Do I recommend this PS converted PC game. Yes, they did a great job and you really do not need a controller.

Highly recommended 9/10

A few of the collectibles and the jokes were hard to come across without a guide - but it was a fun game to complete. I did my first run on Grounded difficulty and can say this is how the game should be played (based on playing on an easier difficulties for my achievement runs afterwards). Besides a bit of a crunch on one of the last parts of the bonus story, it wasn't too difficult.

It is basically a cliche "escort immune person who can save the world" game - but the characters are likable enough that you can feel immersed in the story. The enemies were interesting, but I felt once you learnt their tricks they became a bit too easy to deal with, and that there could have been a little bit more variety.

It's been on my bucket list for a while to play this, and been taking up storage space for a long time. So I'm happy to finally be able to free up some room for something else.

Hell yeah, short and sweet. i love this game so much not as much as rdr2 but man 35 hours for 2 playthroughs and all trophys. story is amazing has lots of lore that i would like to learn about. like in between  the prolouge and the apocolypse. but the trophys are perfect and even if u are not a trophy collecter u could still get them all. amazing characters and story. 100% recommend getting this and getting all trophys. 10/10
fun game, fun gun play, interesting story and setting
Visually stunning, captivating story, easy controls. Game crashes a lot.
Guys play the game with the helium modification. It's hilarious whenever Ellie or Joel speak.

Experiencing The Last of Us Part I for the very first time through this remake is a surreal experience. It completely transcends the typical video game medium to deliver a cinematic, heart-pounding story about loss, survival, and love. The chemistry and evolving bond between Joel and Ellie are incredibly genuine, making you fiercely attached to their survival. The gameplay is a tense, scrappy mix of stealth and resource management where every single bullet matters. Sneaking past terrifying Clickers and outsmarting human hunters keeps your hands sweating the entire time. If you want a story-driven game that will stay with you long after the credits roll, look no further.

Great game, always wanted to play it back when it was a console exclusive and I don't think the pc Port dissapoints despite the performance and stability issues.
I've had multiple crashes and issues with this despite that it's years old already.
I enjoyed the game and the story very much, The visuals are gorgeous.

Great game, fantastic and interesting world. 
Love hearing the characters when they are out of breath, running away from clickers or danger.
Great game. Scenario, gameplay and visuals.

SOO PEAKKKK

I love post apocalyptic media so I was dying to play this game. I fell in love with the story and the characters. I'm currently doing my second play through and love it just as much as I did the first time.

Just about as close to a 10/10 game as you can get. Unfortunately, I already knew most of the plot because I saw the show first, but I still enjoyed this so much, pretty much every element of it is flawless. One aspect I didn't expect to like so much was the gunplay, it's very accuracy based, and when paired with limited ammo (I was playing survivor) then it gives everything a sense of danger and makes taking down even just a group of thugs very satisfying.
This game is amazing, the story, graphics, horror, the enviroment, i love it.

I came upon wanting to play the game after watching the show. I watched it as it was coming out three years ago and absolutely loved it. I've rewatched it many times and for a long time wanted to try out the game. I've now had the chance to play the game and wow, it's an incredible experience. Both the show and the game stand on their own with their similarities, but especially their differences and how they approach the story and the journey. I loved playing the game. It's well written. The game doesn't feel repetitive nor does it make you feel stuck in this linear path, even if it's a story game. I will be playing it again for sure. I will also be playing Part II for sure, and for anyone not sure if they should get it, i fully recommend it. It's a great game on its own. It stands out from the show, yet both enhance each other's experiences. Thank you for this game, and I'm glad to play it 13 years later after its release.
Great game, very gripping and engaging story.
I love the story and gameplay as well as combat mechanics

The Last of Us Part I deserves all the praise it gets.

The relationship between Joel and Ellie is what truly makes the game unforgettable. The world is brutal and heartbreaking, but the quieter moments between characters are what stuck with me the most.

Even years later, the atmosphere, storytelling, performances, and soundtrack still feel incredibly powerful. Every area feels lived in, abandoned, and believable in a way very few games manage to pull off.

It’s not just a great zombie game. It’s one of the most emotionally immersive games I’ve played.

This a really fun, really scary game, but I really appreciate the accessibility features that the developers have incorporated into this game so that those who do not have great coordination or reflexes can still play and enjoy this game, even with a lowish-end computer!
Amazing game, the beginning is a little difficult but after one hour you are fully into it. I recommend especially after having watched the TV series. You feel completely immersed in the story.
if you heard about how the port first came out it is a ton better now but you NEED upscaling on a AMD GPU. other than that the game is hella worth it.
great remaster, graphics glitched a few time and game crashed once but overall it's great
So far it's a pretty good game, but poorly optimized for PC, stuttering happens all the time

Story-driven game with a powerful emotional journey between Joel and Ellie. The visuals, atmosphere, and voice acting make the world feel painfully real. It is a story about survival, trauma, love, and the choices people make when they are afraid of losing someone again.

10/10 the amazing graphics with the amazing and timeless story makes this game amazing, but only pick it up on sale, because bugs aren't uncommon, and because only $30 for all of this? amazing. would recommend

one of the best story games there is, runs smoothly, nothing bad to say about it. however, the achievements are really ass (like wdym you get an achievement for not giving ellie a high-five??? why not have achievements for completing chapters or certain events instead? i dont get that) and they're also quite buggy. aside from that it's perfect.

Decided to give this game a try. And I liked it. The level of detail is very cool, light and shadows are creating very stark/foreboding landscapes. I'm neutral on the story tbh, I play for engrossment. Ellie is highly annoying in the beginning, but she becomes somewhat decent during the progression. Joel carries the show for me, just does what is needed to get done. A coincidence of sorts, I'm listening to an audiobook which has a similar premise, only with one huge change, the infected do not mutate, they just go crazy and die off. Really torn about the TLOU2, since it is soaked in the current agenda nonsense.
This game has been out sense 2013, and no one told me how ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ good it is. I'm here to tell how good this game is, so it doesn't happen to you.

TLOU!!! I first played this game back in 2015 with my ps3. Since then,  I've not played nor seen anything related to the The last of Us series. This remake for PC brought that fixation of 2015 back to 2026, the graphic work, soundtrack and the DLC are fabulous and would lure any fan or new player back to enjoy the experience. However, sometimes during my playthrough i experienced glitching of some textures due to the downloading shaders, sometimes the game crashes and the achievement "skillz" in left behind is bugged af. Overall, an awesome game im so glad i could play again.

The Last of Us Part I is a fantastic game overall. The story is excellent, emotional, and keeps you invested from start to finish. Some sections can be pretty challenging, which made the gameplay feel rewarding without becoming frustrating.

The gameplay is solid, controls feel good, combat and exploration are fun, and going for 100% achievements is surprisingly manageable and enjoyable.

Left Behind is also a great addition — it adds more emotional depth to the characters and fits naturally into the main experience.

Visually, the game looks amazing, and the atmosphere is top tier.

My only downside: I experienced a few freezes during my playthrough. Nothing that ruined the game for me, but definitely worth mentioning.

Overall, a great game and an easy recommendation. I enjoyed it a lot.
Greatest game ever made. Graphics 10/10 Story 10/10 i love this franchise so much
Cinematic masterpiece. The gameplay has an excellent pace and this is the most beautiful game I've played so far to play with HDR enabled.
Captivating story, believable characters, great music and worldbuilding. Gameplay is really fun, too.

LITERALLY MY FAVORITE FRANCHISE OF ALL TIME!!!!!!! The game mechanics are great and the graphics are amazing there is so much to love about this game im sure you will find something to love to if you try it.
I only bought this for benchmarking, but the story and gameplay got me hooked.

I have played through The Last of Us across multiple playthroughs and multiple different consoles. It is one of my favorite games of all time. The storytelling, the world building, and the acting are all top notch, especially for a game that is essentially 13 years old at this point. I don't disagree with many people's issues about the game being a "Playstation walking sim" where there are moments in which the story supersedes the gameplay. It is present and can be a hinderance. Despite that fact, this is one of the few games that I really come to play exclusively for the narrative. When you get really invested in the characters and their journeys, it becomes one of the most engrossing narrative experiences across any medium. Many will put up their finger with a "but..." as soon as you mention the show or the sequel video game. Thankfully, this game is a wonderfully complete package with a gorgeous presentation. I had some small issues with the game 'crashing' where all I had to do was verify game files and restart, which took all of 5 minutes, but the bugs that hurt this release back in the day seem to be a thing of the past. Biggest downside for Part 1 is the lack of Factions MP that was on the original release. That was one of my favorite multiplayer experiences that will probably never make it to PC.

Highly recommended for fans of the stealth genre and thrilling stories.

Last of us main story: 9/10

Also the gameplay is so fun the stealth sections were actually satisfying to pull off and every combat scenario with all the diff guns and tools at your disposal felt fun and new I remember playing this game on the ps4 and loving it and this remake with its beautiful graphics one up'ed my original experince entirely

The story is the most captivating campaign of all time, hands down my favourite game ever. I got goosebumps several times, had to fight back tears and got my heart broken more by the game than any girl ever. The atmosphere is insane, and you never know what to expect next. The character design and world building is next level. The music and exploration is captivating. I would tell anyone to play this game

I just gotta know, why Joel???

This game is definitely up there on my top 3 games of all time. The story is unlike any game I have played before and was the one game I have gotten seriously emotional over. The game was the game that really set the scene and showed how impactful video games can be. The game has been remade wonderfully, keeping the same atmosphere and gameplay as before but giving it a HUGE overhaul and has been ported to PC wonderfully. This game is the only game that I’d say EVERYONE needs to play at least once

SO good dude like story is insane and graphics are insane its just an insanely good game
The Last of Us in all its PC remastered glory and just as good as always.

Amazing from start to finish. As an urban explorer, I was blown away by the atmosphere... It captures that same sense of wonder and eerie beauty I find when exploring abandoned places in Japan. The writing is perfect, the visuals are stunning, and the sound design is flawless. It’s a beautiful, immersive journey. Ready for Part II!

The Last of Us feels even better after finishing Part II. Going back to the first game made me realize how strong the character writing really was, especially for Joel and Ellie. Their relationship develops naturally throughout the journey, from strangers who barely trust each other to people who genuinely care about one another like family. After knowing what happens later in Part II, many moments in the first game feel much more emotional and meaningful.

One of the best parts of the game is how realistic the world feels. The disaster setting is done incredibly well. The abandoned cities, empty streets, ruined buildings, and quiet atmosphere make the world feel truly broken. It doesn’t just look like a post-apocalyptic world — it feels like people actually lived there before everything collapsed. Small details, like notes left behind by survivors or destroyed homes, make the environment tell its own story.

The gameplay still holds up too. The combat is simple compared to Part II, but it feels tense because resources are limited and every encounter can be dangerous. The soundtrack and voice acting also help create a heavy emotional atmosphere that stays with you even after finishing the game.

Overall, replaying The Last of Us after Part II made me appreciate it even more. The story, characters, and world-building are some of the best in gaming, and it’s easy to see why so many people still consider it a classic.
Story is great, gameplay is great. Some parts are a bit slow but it gives you time to appreciate the narrative so far as well as the environment and character interactions.
Well the game is good but it could be way better considering certain things.. for example there is no mini map ...it does not give hints

The Last of Us Part I is pure atmosphere — every quiet moment hits just as hard as the action.
Beautiful storytelling, curated music, and visuals that make the world feel alive.
One of those games that stays with you.

This game has an excellent story and, in my opinion, amazing replayability. The only issue with this game is the insanely high price. Wait for it to go on discount if you can, or be like me and spend all your money.

Overall a pretty good game. I played it back when it first came to the PS3. I remember the overall story so I played part 2 first but then decided to come back to play this.

A few things I don't understand: why is it so much money? And secondly, each game session it would randomly do this strange 10 second pause where the sound keeps playing but the game froze. After waiting it comes back to life and everything is okay. It seems to happen only once per game session and after it runs perfectly fine. I don't recall this ever happening with part 2 but I would expect for the full price that this game would run similar to part 2.

With that said, it is only a minor hiccup in the game. I was able to enjoy it from start to finish and as I said once the weird pause happens, the game runs smoothly after no matter how long you play until you exit out and start the game again. The game ran very smoothly and the game play is smooth.
a beautifully remade port for a touching and heartwrenching game.
It's always great to play this game again visually upgraded. it's still breathtaking still one of my favorite franchise.

Conoci el juego por Vegetta777, vi todo lo que hizo en el juego, luego me vi la serie con mi ahora ex novia, y queria transmitirle el juego en algun momento, pero supongo andaba muy ocupada ''avanzando'' ella sola y no se dio cuenta que retrocedi por ella para avanzar juntos.

You know what? I'm pleasantly suprised how good this game it. Pacing. Combat. Dialogue. Overaching story. It all fits togother. I was deeply invested in Joel and Ellie's journey across the US, and how their behaviour can be understood, excused and debated. I was also impressed by how much of the game actually made it into the HBO series. They were good elements and scenes, so there was -- I suppose -- no reason not to use them.

There were some things that made me go "Ah, AAA game from 2013", like the showcasing of impressive (but perhaps not entirely necessery) mechanics like swimming, box movement, horseback riding, etc. They are necessery to fill time and create pauses inbetween action sequences, but I can't avoid viewing them as game elements that needed to be reused throughout the game.

[h1]The Game That Changed Naughty Dog Forever.[/h1]

[h2]Introduction:[/h2]
In 2013, Naughty Dog released The Last of Us for the PlayStation 3. As a fan of the Uncharted series growing up, and someone who already admired Naughty Dog for their work on that franchise, I remember being 10 years old and begging my mom to get it for me—and she did. Thanks, Mom.
At the time, the game genuinely terrified me. It was unlike anything I had experienced before and offered a glimpse into a darker, more mature type of storytelling. Back then, I didn’t fully understand the father-daughter dynamic in the way I do now at 23. I revisited the remastered version and even played the opening segment of The Last of Us Part I on the PlayStation 5 a few years ago, but never made it more than an hour in. This year, however, I committed to taking the story slowly—fully immersing myself in the world and experiencing it one final time.

[h2]Story:[/h2]
The story still holds up remarkably well, delivering one of the most emotional and grounded portrayals of humanity in any video game, in my opinion. As you progress, you develop a deeper understanding of Joel and Ellie, watching their relationship evolve over time.
The game begins at a slower pace, focusing on character development, but once you reach Pittsburgh, the intensity ramps up significantly. Along the way, you encounter Sam and Henry, whose relationship mirrors that of Joel and Ellie, reinforcing the game’s central themes of trust and survival. Through this, Joel gradually begins to trust Ellie more, influenced by Henry’s bond with Sam. However, their time together is short-lived, reinforcing the harsh and unpredictable nature of the world.
The game excels at environmental storytelling, using its world design alongside a haunting soundtrack to build an eerie, immersive atmosphere—especially in abandoned suburban areas where life has come to a complete standstill. By the end of the journey, Joel no longer sees Ellie as mere “cargo,” but as something far more meaningful. This shift in perspective hit differently this time around, making the experience feel fresh again. There were several moments that felt genuinely emotional, highlighting the beauty found within their struggle.

[h2]Gameplay:[/h2]
The gameplay has seen noticeable improvements from the original release, though some flaws remain. Combat is still intense and brutal, with enhanced gore and improved AI behavior. While the AI does not quite reach the level seen in The Last of Us Part II, it comes close, and the overall experience feels more refined.
That said, certain mechanics still feel dated. Movement can be somewhat stiff—likely an intentional design choice—but at times it reinforces the game’s 2013 origins. Puzzles such as retrieving pallets for Ellie to cross water or repositioning dumpsters to reach higher areas remain as repetitive and frustrating as ever.

[h2]Graphics and Performance:[/h2]
When it originally launched on the PlayStation 3, The Last of Us was already visually impressive. Now, it has been elevated to a near-photorealistic level, standing alongside The Last of Us Part II in terms of visual fidelity. The lighting, textures, and attention to detail significantly enhance immersion.
I played on the highest settings in 4K and experienced only a single crash. However, I did notice occasional lighting inconsistencies, along with some noticeable frame rate drops in certain areas.
Left Behind DLC:
The Left Behind DLC follows Ellie and Riley before the main events of The Last of Us. The story flashes back and forth between what happens while Joel is injured in the main story during the Winter segment, and as I said, Ellie and Riley. There isn’t much action in this DLC, it is mainly story based, similar to the main story. It’s about building a connection between Ellie and Riley and showing how much the two care for each other. The DLC isn’t a must play, but it does show motives for Ellie’s light for survival and how she became the person she is in the main game. 

[h2]Final Thoughts:[/h2]
As I mentioned at the start, The Last of Us is what shaped Naughty Dog into who they are today as a game developer. This game is certainly a must play. The gameplay and mechanics may be aging, but the story telling that is present here is timeless. 

[h3]Rating:[/h3]
[b]9/10 | Amazing[/b]

I've only got 2.9 hours on the pc version, but I've beaten it twice on PS5. Legitimately one of the greatest story games of all time, the characters feel so real, the environment is amazing, the atmosphere is beyond amazing. Genuine 10/10 game.

So far on the pc version I haven't met any performance issues at all.

If you've never played this, I'm jealous that you get to experience it for the first time.

Awesome game with an amazing story and gripping gameplay. The environments are beautiful with world-class attention to detail. Enemies are smart and unpredictable. Collectibles and artefacts litter the world urging you to look around every corner and in every nook.

A game you can sink countless hours into. It also has great replay value IMO, as it's a totally different experience on the harder difficulties.
such a great game with captivating gameplay! you get sucked into it and don't want to put it down.

The Last of Us is a great game with an amazing story. I watched the first season on HBO, but I honestly think the game is much better. Playing through Joel and Ellie’s journey makes the story feel more emotional and personal than the series
good game optimised and everything w game from naugthydog
Great game love the grpahics run it pretty high barely lagged i really reccomend

The Last of Us Part 1 is a 10/10 for me.

This game came into my life when I needed it most. I gave it a try after years of being interested at one of the darkest periods ive ever been through. At the time, I was struggling with drug abuse, and honestly, I came dangerously close to losing my life. I also lost people that I loved because of it. This game gave me something to hold onto like a light or a raft in the middle of an ocean of black. It helped pull my focus away from that and helped me look forward to the future and appreciate the moment. 

The story, the characters, the music, and the atmosphere all connected with me. Joel and Ellies journey gave me an escape when I needed one, but it also reminded me to keep moving forward. Ive played it through and perfected it twice. Its about survival, love, loss and its the best Story driven game ive ever played.
Great gameplay, good graphics, and an okay story. Worth playing if you have never experienced it. Wait for a sale you don't really want to pay full price for a remaster of a remaster of a 2013 game.

One of the best level designs. 10/10 animations. Really cool sounds. But the story is so predictable and simple.
12 hours you are going to shoot people and some zombies. For only 2-3 hours you see how story is going. The best part of the game is the winter season with really cool gameplay and you feel like you are trying to survive. I felt nothing when i finished the game. I have been trying to finish the Left Behind DLC and it really hard to do. DLC does not make feel anything cause all i know about Riley (Ellie's friend) that she is a a Firefly and gonna die. The flashbacks with Riley lasted too long for a character whose only known trait was that she died.

A brilliant game with excellent storytelling and presentation. I especially liked the world-building, enemy behavior, and side characters, all of it adds a lot to the immersion. Back in 2013, it was a masterpiece, and in my opinion, it still remains one. It still feels like the benchmark for a linear game with a cinematic story and presentation
